subject: Mathematics
q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>a</mi><mi>r</mi></msub></math> is the coefficient of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mrow><mn>10</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>r</mi></mrow></msup></math> in the Binomial expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>10</mn></msup></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>r</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>10</mn></munderover><msup><mi>r</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><msup><mfenced><mfrac><msub><mi>a</mi><mi>r</mi></msub><msub><mi>a</mi><mrow><mi>r</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></mfrac></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>a</mi><mi>r</mi></msub></math> is the coefficient of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mrow><mn>10</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>r</mi></mrow></msup></math> in the Binomial expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>10</mn></msup></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>r</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>10</mn></munderover><msup><mi>r</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><msup><mfenced><mfrac><msub><mi>a</mi><mi>r</mi></msub><msub><mi>a</mi><mrow><mi>r</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></mfrac></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to
solution:
Since, ar is the coefficient of x10-r in the binomial expansion of 1+x10, therefore

ar=C10-r10=Cr10

ar-1=Cr-110

So,

arar-1=Cr10Cr-110

⇒arar-1=10!r!×10-r!×r-1!×11-r!10!

⇒arar-1=11-rr

Now,

∑r=110r3arar-12=∑r=110r311-rr2

⇒∑r=110r3arar-12=∑r=110r11-r2

⇒∑r=110r3arar-12=∑r=110r121+r2-22r

⇒∑r=110r3arar-12=∑r=110121r+r3-22r2

⇒∑r=110r3arar-12=121×10×112+10×1122-22×10×11×216

⇒∑r=110r3arar-12=6655+3025-8470

⇒∑r=110r3arar-12=1210

subject: Mathematics
q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>c</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mn>10</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>i</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn></mrow><mn>20</mn></munderover><msub><mi>p</mi><mi>i</mi></msub><msup><mi>x</mi><mi>i</mi></msup><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>c</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">ℕ</mi></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>p</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>20</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>p</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>210</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>c</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>c</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mn>10</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>i</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn></mrow><mn>20</mn></munderover><msub><mi>p</mi><mi>i</mi></msub><msup><mi>x</mi><mi>i</mi></msup><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>c</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">ℕ</mi></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>p</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>20</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>p</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>210</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>c</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
Given that a+bx+cx210=∑i=1020pixi, a, b, c∈ℕ

General term : 10!r1! r2! r3!ar1bxr2cx2r3

For coefficient. of x:r2+2r3=1

Let r1r2r3910

∴ coefficient of x=10!9!a9b1=20

⇒a9·b=2 ......i

Coefficient of ⇒x2:10!8! 2! 0!a8·b2+10!9! 0! 1!·a9·c=210

⇒45a8·b2+10·a9·c=210

⇒9a8b2+2a9·c=42 .......ii

As a, b, c∈N, so by hit and trial in equation i & ii we get, a=1, b=2, c=3

Hence, 2a+b+c=23+2+1=12

subject: Mathematics
q: Let the coefficient of xr in the expansion of x+3n−1+x+3n−2x+2+x+3n−3x+22+....+x+2n−1 be αr. If∑r=0nαr=βn−γn,β,γ∈N, then the value of β2+γ2 equals _______.
question: Let the coefficient of xr in the expansion of x+3n−1+x+3n−2x+2+x+3n−3x+22+....+x+2n−1 be αr. If∑r=0nαr=βn−γn,β,γ∈N, then the value of β2+γ2 equals _______.
solution:
Let,

S=x+3n−1+x+3n−2x+2+x+3n−3x+22+.....+x+2n−1

Now, using the sum of G.P formula we get,

⇒S=x+3n-11-x+2x+3n1-x+2x+3

⇒S=x+3n-x+2n

Now, finding the sum of coefficients we get,

⇒∑αr=∑x+3n-∑x+2n

⇒4n−3n=βn−γn by taking x=1

So, on comparing both side we get, β=4, γ=3

Hence, β2+γ2=16+9=25

subject: Mathematics
q: Suppose A and B are the coefficients of $30^{\text {th }}$ and $12^{\text {th }}$ terms respectively in the binomial expansion of $(1+x)^{2 \mathrm{n}-1}$. If $2 \mathrm{~A}=5 \mathrm{~B}$, then n is equal to :
question: Suppose A and B are the coefficients of $30^{\text {th }}$ and $12^{\text {th }}$ terms respectively in the binomial expansion of $(1+x)^{2 \mathrm{n}-1}$. If $2 \mathrm{~A}=5 \mathrm{~B}$, then n is equal to :
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{A}={ }^{2 \mathrm{n}-1} \mathrm{C}_{29} \quad \mathrm{~B}={ }^{2 \mathrm{n}-1} \mathrm{C}_{11} \\ & 2^{2 \mathrm{n}-1} \mathrm{C}_{29}=5{ }^{2 \mathrm{n}-1} \mathrm{C}_{11} \\ & 2 \frac{(2 \mathrm{n}-1)!}{29!(2 \mathrm{n}-30)!}=5 \frac{(2 \mathrm{n}-1)!}{(2 \mathrm{n}-12)!11!} \\ & \frac{1}{29 \ldots 12 \cdot 5}=\frac{1}{(2 \mathrm{n}-12)(2 \mathrm{n}-13) \ldots(2 \mathrm{n}-29) 2} \\ & \frac{1}{30 \cdot 29 \ldots 12}=\frac{1}{(2 \mathrm{n}-12)(2 \mathrm{n}-13) \ldots(2 \mathrm{n}-29) 12} \\ & 2 \mathrm{n}-12=30 \\ & \mathrm{n}=21\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q:
If b is very small as compared to the value of a, so that the cube and other higher powers of ba can be neglected in the identity

1a-b+1a-2b+1a-3b+….+1a-nb=αn+βn2+γn3

then the value of γ is :
question:
If b is very small as compared to the value of a, so that the cube and other higher powers of ba can be neglected in the identity

1a-b+1a-2b+1a-3b+….+1a-nb=αn+βn2+γn3

then the value of γ is :
solution:
Given,

1a-b+1a-2b+1a-3b+….+1a-nb

=a-b-1+a-2b-1+….+a-nb-1

=1a∑r=1n1-rba-1

=1a∑r=1n1+rba+r2b2a2+terms to be neglected

=1an+n(n+1)2·ba+n(n+1)(2n+1)6·b2a2

=1an+n2+n2·ba+2n3+3n2+n6·b2a2

So γ=b23a3

q:
The value of

11!50!+13!48!+15!46!+….+149!2!+151!1! is
question:
The value of

11!50!+13!48!+15!46!+….+149!2!+151!1! is
solution:
Let

S=11!·50!+13!·48!+15!·46!+....+151!·1!

⇒S=151!51!1!·50!+51!3!·48!+51!5!·46!+....+51!51!·0!

⇒S=151!C151+C351+C551+....+C5151

⇒S=251-151!

⇒S=25051!

subject: Mathematics
q: For some $n \neq 10$, let the coefficients of the 5 th, 6 th and 7 th terms in the binomial expansion of $(1+\mathrm{x})^{\mathrm{n}+4}$ be in A.P. Then the largest coefficient in the expansion of $(1+\mathrm{x})^{\mathrm{n}+4}$ is:
question: For some $n \neq 10$, let the coefficients of the 5 th, 6 th and 7 th terms in the binomial expansion of $(1+\mathrm{x})^{\mathrm{n}+4}$ be in A.P. Then the largest coefficient in the expansion of $(1+\mathrm{x})^{\mathrm{n}+4}$ is:
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & (1+x)^{n+4} \\ & { }^{n+4} C_4,{ }^{n+4} C_5,{ }^{n+4} C_6, \rightarrow \text { A.P. } \\ & \Rightarrow 2 \times { }^{n+4} C_5={ }^{n+4} C_4+{ }^{n+4} C_6 \\ & \Rightarrow 4 \times{ }^{n+4} C_5=\left({ }^{n+4} C_4+{ }^{n+4} C_5\right)+\left({ }^{n+4} C_5+{ }^{n+4} C_6\right) \\ & \Rightarrow 4 \times{ }^{n+4} C_5={ }^{n+5} C_5+{ }^{n+5} C_6\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow 4 \times \frac{(\mathrm{n}+4)!}{5!\cdot(\mathrm{n}-1)!}=\frac{(\mathrm{n}+6)!}{6!\cdot \mathrm{n}!} \\ & \Rightarrow 4=\frac{(\mathrm{n}+6)(\mathrm{n}+5)}{6 \mathrm{n}} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{n}^2+11 \mathrm{n}+30=24 \mathrm{n} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{n}^2-13 \mathrm{n}+30=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{n}=3,10(\text { rejected }) \\ & \because \mathrm{n} \neq 10\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ Largest binomial coefficient in expansion of
$\begin{aligned}
& (1+\mathrm{x})^7 \\
& (\because \mathrm{n}+4=7)
\end{aligned}$
is coeff. of middle term
$\Rightarrow{ }^7 \mathrm{C}_4={ }^7 \mathrm{C}_3=35$

subject: Mathematics
q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>k</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>31</mn></munderover><mfenced><mmultiscripts><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi>k</mi><none></none><mprescripts></mprescripts><none></none><mn>31</mn></mmultiscripts></mfenced><mfenced><mmultiscripts><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mrow><mi>k</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><none></none><mprescripts></mprescripts><none></none><mn>31</mn></mmultiscripts></mfenced><mo>-</mo><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>k</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>30</mn></munderover><mfenced><mmultiscripts><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi>k</mi><none></none><mprescripts></mprescripts><none></none><mn>30</mn></mmultiscripts></mfenced><mfenced><mmultiscripts><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mrow><mi>k</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><none></none><mprescripts></mprescripts><none></none><mn>30</mn></mmultiscripts></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>α</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>60</mn><mo>!</mo></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mfenced><mrow><mn>30</mn><mo>!</mo></mrow></mfenced><mfenced><mrow><mn>31</mn><mo>!</mo></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfrac></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>16</mn><mi>α</mi></math> is equal to
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>k</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>31</mn></munderover><mfenced><mmultiscripts><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi>k</mi><none></none><mprescripts></mprescripts><none></none><mn>31</mn></mmultiscripts></mfenced><mfenced><mmultiscripts><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mrow><mi>k</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><none></none><mprescripts></mprescripts><none></none><mn>31</mn></mmultiscripts></mfenced><mo>-</mo><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>k</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>30</mn></munderover><mfenced><mmultiscripts><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi>k</mi><none></none><mprescripts></mprescripts><none></none><mn>30</mn></mmultiscripts></mfenced><mfenced><mmultiscripts><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mrow><mi>k</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><none></none><mprescripts></mprescripts><none></none><mn>30</mn></mmultiscripts></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>α</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>60</mn><mo>!</mo></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mfenced><mrow><mn>30</mn><mo>!</mo></mrow></mfenced><mfenced><mrow><mn>31</mn><mo>!</mo></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfrac></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>16</mn><mi>α</mi></math> is equal to
solution:
∑k=131Ck31·Ck-131

=C131·C031+C231·C131+⋯+C3131·C3031

=C031·C3031+C131·C2931+⋯+C3031·C031 (using Crn=Cr-1n)

=C3062 (using C0n·Cpm+C1n·Cp-1m+⋯+Cpn·C0m=Cpm+n)

Similarly,

∑k=130Ck30·Ck-130=C2960

C3062-C2960=62!30!32!-60!29!31!

=60!29!31!62·6130·32-1

=60!30!31!282232

So α=282232

∴ 16α=16×282232=1411

subject: Mathematics
q:
Let $\alpha, \beta, \gamma$ and $\delta$ be the coefficients of $x^7, x^5, x^3$ and $x$ respectively in the expansion of $\left(x+\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)^5+\left(x-\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)^5, x\gt1$. If u and v satisfy the equations
$\begin{aligned}
& \alpha u+\beta v=18 \\
& \gamma u+\delta v=20
\end{aligned}$
then $u+v$ equals :
question:
Let $\alpha, \beta, \gamma$ and $\delta$ be the coefficients of $x^7, x^5, x^3$ and $x$ respectively in the expansion of $\left(x+\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)^5+\left(x-\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)^5, x\gt1$. If u and v satisfy the equations
$\begin{aligned}
& \alpha u+\beta v=18 \\
& \gamma u+\delta v=20
\end{aligned}$
then $u+v$ equals :
solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \left(x+\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)^5+\left(x-\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)^5 \\
& -\left[{ }^5 C_0 x^5+{ }^5 C_1 x^4\left(\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)+\ldots \ldots+{ }^5 C_5\left(\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)^5\right]+ \\
& {\left[{ }^5 C_0 x^5-{ }^5 C_1 x^4\left(\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)+\ldots \ldots .+{ }^{-5} C_5\left(\sqrt{x^3-1}\right)^5\right]} \\
& =2\left[x^5+{ }^5 C_2 x^3\left(x^3-1\right)+{ }^5 C_4\left(x^3-1\right)^2\right] \\
& =10 x^7+20 x^6+2 x^5-20 x^4-20 x^3+10 x
\end{aligned}$

Now
$\alpha=10, \beta=2, \gamma=-20, \delta=10$

Also,
$\begin{aligned}
& \left.\begin{array}{c}
10 u+2 v=18 \\
-20 u+10 v=20
\end{array}\right\} u=1, v=4 \\
& u+v=5
\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q: If for some <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>n</mi><mo>;</mo><msup><mo> </mo><mn>6</mn></msup><msub><mi>C</mi><mi>m</mi></msub><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mfenced><mrow><msup><mrow></mrow><mn>6</mn></msup><msub><mi>C</mi><mrow><mi>m</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced><msup><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn></msup><msub><mi>C</mi><mrow><mi>m</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msup><mo>></mo><mn>8</mn></msup><msub><mi>C</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mrow></mrow><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><msup><mo>:</mo><mi>n</mi></msup><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>8</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mrow></mrow><mi>n</mi></msup><msub><mi>P</mi><mrow><mi>m</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><msup><mo>+</mo><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><msub><mi>C</mi><mi>m</mi></msub></math> is equal to
question: If for some <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>n</mi><mo>;</mo><msup><mo> </mo><mn>6</mn></msup><msub><mi>C</mi><mi>m</mi></msub><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mfenced><mrow><msup><mrow></mrow><mn>6</mn></msup><msub><mi>C</mi><mrow><mi>m</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced><msup><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn></msup><msub><mi>C</mi><mrow><mi>m</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msup><mo>></mo><mn>8</mn></msup><msub><mi>C</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mrow></mrow><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><msup><mo>:</mo><mi>n</mi></msup><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>8</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mrow></mrow><mi>n</mi></msup><msub><mi>P</mi><mrow><mi>m</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><msup><mo>+</mo><mrow><mi>n</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><msub><mi>C</mi><mi>m</mi></msub></math> is equal to
solution:
Given: 6Cm+26Cm+1+6Cm+2>8C3

⇒6Cm+6Cm+1+6Cm+1+6Cm+2>8C3

⇒7Cm+1+7Cm+2>8C3

⇒8Cm+2>8C3

⇒m=2

Also, n−1P3:nP4=1:8

⇒n−1n−2n−3nn−1n−2n−3=18

⇒n=8

⇒nPm+1+n+1Cm=8P3+9C2

⇒nPm+1+n+1Cm=8×7×6+9×82

⇒nPm+1+n+1Cm=372

subject: Mathematics
q: If $n={ }^m C_2$, then the value of ${ }^n C_2$ is given by
question: If $n={ }^m C_2$, then the value of ${ }^n C_2$ is given by
solution: $n={ }^m \mathrm{C}_2=\frac{m(m-1)}{2}$ Since $m$ and $(m-1)$ are two consecutive natural numbers, therefore their product is an even natural number. So $\frac{m(m-1)}{2}$ is also a natural number. Now $\frac{m(m-1)}{2}=\frac{m^2-m}{2}$ $$ \begin{aligned} \therefore & \frac{m(m-1)}{2} \mathrm{C}_2=\frac{\left(\frac{m^2-m}{2}\right)\left(\frac{m^2-m}{2}-1\right)}{2} \\ & =\frac{m(m-1)\left(m^2-m-2\right)}{8} \\ & =\frac{m(m-1)\left[m^2-2 m+m-2\right]}{8} \\ & =\frac{m(m-1)[m(m-2)+1(m-2)]}{8} \\ & =\frac{m(m-1)(m-2)(m+1)}{8} \end{aligned} $$

subject: Mathematics
q:
Among the statements :

(S1) : 20232022-19992022 is divisible by 8.

(S2) : 13(13)n-11n-13 is divisible by 144 for infinitely many n∈ℕ
question:
Among the statements :

(S1) : 20232022-19992022 is divisible by 8.

(S2) : 13(13)n-11n-13 is divisible by 144 for infinitely many n∈ℕ
solution:
Given,

(S1) : (2023)2022-(1999)2022 is divisible by 8

Now we know that (x-y) divides xn-yn ∀n∈ℕ

So, 2023-1999 divides (2023)2022-(1999)2022

⇒ 24 divides (2023)2022-(1999)2002

⇒ 8 will divide (2023)2022-(1999)2002

As 8 divides 24

Hence, S1 is correct

Now solving,

S2 : 1313n-11n-13 is divisible by 144 for n∈ℕ

So using binomial theorem in 1+12n we get,

131+12n-11n-13

=13nC0+nC112+nC2122+...+nCn12n-11n-13

=12×13n-11n+122λ

=145n+144λ which is not divisible by 144

Hence, S2 is incorrect

subject: Mathematics
q: If the co-efficient of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>9</mn></msup></math> in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mi>β</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mn>11</mn></msup></math>and the co-efficient of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>9</mn></mrow></msup></math> in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mi>β</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mn>11</mn></msup></math> are equal, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>α</mi><mi>β</mi><msup><mo>)</mo><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to
question: If the co-efficient of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>9</mn></msup></math> in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mi>β</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mn>11</mn></msup></math>and the co-efficient of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>9</mn></mrow></msup></math> in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mi>β</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mn>11</mn></msup></math> are equal, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>α</mi><mi>β</mi><msup><mo>)</mo><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to
solution:
Given,

The coefficient of x9 in αx3+1βx11and the coefficient of x-9 in αx-1βx311 are equal,

Now the rth term of αx3+1βx11 is given by =Cr11·α11-rβr×x311-r-r

So, for x9, 33-4r=9⇒r=6

Hence, coefficient of x9 in αx3+1βx11=C611·α5β6

Similarly, for coefficient of x-9 in αx-1βx311=-C511·α6β5

Now given both coefficient are equal,

So, 11C6·α5β6=-11C5·α6β5

⇒1β=-α

⇒αβ=-1

⇒(αβ)2=1

solution:
Given, (27)999

=28-19997

=28λ-17

=28λ-7+7-17

=74λ-1+67

∴ Remainder=6

q: The coefficient of <math> <mrow> <msup> <mrow> <mi></mi><mi></mi><mi></mi><mi></mi><mi></mi><mi>x</mi><mo>⁡</mo> </mrow> <mrow> <mn>1</mn> <mn>0</mn> <mn>1</mn> <mn>2</mn> </mrow> </msup> </mrow> </math>  in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mi>n</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>253</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mn>0</mn></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo></math> (where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi><mo>≤22</mo></math> is any positive integer), is
question: The coefficient of <math> <mrow> <msup> <mrow> <mi></mi><mi></mi><mi></mi><mi></mi><mi></mi><mi>x</mi><mo>⁡</mo> </mrow> <mrow> <mn>1</mn> <mn>0</mn> <mn>1</mn> <mn>2</mn> </mrow> </msup> </mrow> </math>  in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mi>n</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>253</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mn>0</mn></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo></math> (where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi><mo>≤22</mo></math> is any positive integer), is
solution:
Given 1+xn+x25310

=1+x253+xn10

Using the binomial expansion a+bn=C0nanb0+C1nan-1b+C2nan-2b2+...+Cnna0bn,

=10C01+x25310xn0+10C11+x2539xn1+10C21+x2538xn2+...+10C101+x2530xn10

As  2 5 3 = 2 3 × 1 1  and 1012=253×4, also n≤22

⇒ Coefficient of x1012 will come only from the first term, i.e. in

10C01+x25310xn0=1+x25310

The general term in the expansion of 1+an is Tr+1=Crnar

Hence, the general term in the expansion of 1+x25310 is Tr+1=Cr10x253r=Cr10x253r

Since, 1012=253×4, hence r=4

Thus, the required coefficient is=10C4.

subject: Mathematics
q: For natural numbers $m, n$ if $(1-y)^m(1+y)^n=1+a_1 y+a_2 y^2+\ldots$, and $a_1=a_2=10$ then $(\mathrm{m}, \mathrm{n})$ is
question: For natural numbers $m, n$ if $(1-y)^m(1+y)^n=1+a_1 y+a_2 y^2+\ldots$, and $a_1=a_2=10$ then $(\mathrm{m}, \mathrm{n})$ is
solution:
$(1-y)^m(1+y)^n=\left[1-{ }^m C_1 y+{ }^m C_2 y^2-\ldots\right]\left[1+{ }^n C_1 y+{ }^n C_2 y^2+\ldots\right]$
$=1+(n-m)+\left\{\frac{m(m-1)}{2}+\frac{n(n-1)}{2}-m n\right\} y^2+\ldots .$.
$\therefore a_1=n-m=10$ and $a_2=\frac{m^2+n-m-n-2 m n}{2}=10$
So, $n-m=10$ and $(m-n)^2-(m+n)=20 \quad \Rightarrow m+n=80$
$$
\therefore \mathrm{m}=35, \quad \mathrm{n}=45
$$

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $\mathrm{S}=\frac{1}{25!}+\frac{1}{3!23!}+\frac{1}{5!21!}+\ldots$ up to 13 terms. If $13 \mathrm{~S}=\frac{2^{k}}{n!}, k \in \mathrm{~N}$, then $n+k$ is equal to
question: Let $\mathrm{S}=\frac{1}{25!}+\frac{1}{3!23!}+\frac{1}{5!21!}+\ldots$ up to 13 terms. If $13 \mathrm{~S}=\frac{2^{k}}{n!}, k \in \mathrm{~N}$, then $n+k$ is equal to
solution:
The general term is $\frac{1}{(2r-1)!(27-2r)!}$ for $r=1$ to $13$.
Multiply and divide by $26!$: $\frac{1}{(2r-1)!(27-2r)!} = \frac{1}{26!}\binom{26}{2r-1}$
$S = \frac{1}{26!}\sum_{r=1}^{13}\binom{26}{2r-1} = \frac{1}{26!}\left[\binom{26}{1}+\binom{26}{3}+\cdots+\binom{26}{25}\right]$
Sum of odd-indexed binomial coefficients: $\sum \binom{26}{\text{odd}} = 2^{25}$
$S = \frac{2^{25}}{26!}$
$13S = \frac{13 \cdot 2^{25}}{26!} = \frac{13 \cdot 2^{25}}{26 \cdot 25!} = \frac{2^{25}}{2 \cdot 25!} = \frac{2^{24}}{25!}$
So $k = 24,\; n = 25$, giving $n + k = 49$.

subject: Mathematics
q: If <math><mi>α</mi></math> and <math><mi>β</mi></math>, be the coefficients of <math><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msup></math> and <math><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>, respectively in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msqrt><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt></mrow></mfenced><mn>6</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><msqrt><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt></mrow></mfenced><mn>6</mn></msup></math>, then
question: If <math><mi>α</mi></math> and <math><mi>β</mi></math>, be the coefficients of <math><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msup></math> and <math><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>, respectively in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msqrt><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt></mrow></mfenced><mn>6</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><msqrt><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt></mrow></mfenced><mn>6</mn></msup></math>, then
solution:
x+x2-16+x-x2-16=2C0x6+C2x4x2-1+C4x2x2-12+C6x2-13

=2x6+15x6-x4+15x2x4-2x2+1+-1+3x2-3x4+x6

=232x6-48x4+18x2-1

Now given that α & β are the coefficients of x4 & x2 respectively, hence

α=-96 and β=36

∴α-β=-132

subject: Mathematics
q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>8</mn><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt><mo>+</mo><mn>13</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>13</mn></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>7</mn><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><mo>+</mo><mn>9</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>9</mn></msup></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><mi>t</mi></mfenced></math> denotes the greatest integer <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>≤</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi></math>, then
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>8</mn><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt><mo>+</mo><mn>13</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>13</mn></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>7</mn><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><mo>+</mo><mn>9</mn></mrow></mfenced><mn>9</mn></msup></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><mi>t</mi></mfenced></math> denotes the greatest integer <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>≤</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi></math>, then
options: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> is odd but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mi>y</mi></mfenced></math> is even

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> is even but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mi>y</mi></mfenced></math> is odd

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mi>y</mi></mfenced></math> are both odd
solution:
We know the binomial expansion of a+bn is,

a+bn=C0nanb0+C1nan-1b1+C2nan-2b2+......+Cnna0bn

Using the above expansion,

x=83+1313=C0138313+C1138312131+…

Let x'=83-1313=C0138313-C1138312131+…

So, x-x'=2C113·8312131+C3138310·133…

Therefore, x-x' is an even integer, hence x is even

Now, y=72+99=C09729+C1972891+C2972792+....Now let,y'=(72-9)9=C09(72)9-C19(72)8(9)1+C2972792

So, y-y'=2C1972891+C3972693+…

y-y'= Even integer hence y is even

subject: Mathematics
q: The ratio of the coefficient of $x^{15}$ to the term independent of $x$ in the expansion of $\left(x^2+\frac{2}{x}\right)^{15}$ is:
question: The ratio of the coefficient of $x^{15}$ to the term independent of $x$ in the expansion of $\left(x^2+\frac{2}{x}\right)^{15}$ is:
solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{T}_{r+1}={ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_r\left(x^2\right)^{15-r} \cdot\left(2 x^{-1}\right)^r \\
& ={ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_r \times(2)^r \times x^{30-3 r}
\end{aligned}
$$
For independent term, $30-3 r=0 \Rightarrow r=10$
Hence the term independent of $x$,
$$
\mathrm{T}_{11}={ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_{10} \times(2)^{10}
$$
For term involve $x^{15}, 30-3 r=15 \Rightarrow r=5$
Hence coefficient of $x^{15}={ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_5 \times(2)^5$
Required ratio
$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\frac{{ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_5 \times(2)^5}{{ }^{15} \mathrm{C}_{10} \times(2)^{10}}=\frac{\frac{15 !}{10 ! 5 !}}{\frac{15 !}{5 ! 10 !} \times(2)^5} \\
& =1: 32 \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$

subject: Mathematics
q: If the fourth term in the binomial expansion of <math><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msqrt><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>10</mn></mrow></msub><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac></mrow></msup></msqrt><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>12</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></msup></math> is equal to <math><mn>200</mn><mo>,</mo></math> and <math><mi>x</mi><mo>></mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of <math><mi>x</mi></math> is
question: If the fourth term in the binomial expansion of <math><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msqrt><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>10</mn></mrow></msub><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac></mrow></msup></msqrt><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>12</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></msup></math> is equal to <math><mn>200</mn><mo>,</mo></math> and <math><mi>x</mi><mo>></mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of <math><mi>x</mi></math> is
solution:
General term for the given binomial is
Tr+1= 6Crx11+log10x6-rx112r
Given T4=200, putting r=3
⇒T4= 6C3x11+log10x3x1123=200
⇒x11+log10x32⋅x14=10
⇒x321+log10x+14=10
⇒321+log10x+14log10x=1
⇒x=10,10-4
⇒x=10 ( as x>1)

subject: Mathematics
q: If the greatest value of the term independent of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mi>sin</mi><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>a</mi><mfrac><mrow><mi>cos</mi><mi>α</mi></mrow><mi>x</mi></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mn>10</mn></msup></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mn>10</mn><mo>!</mo></mrow><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>5</mn><mo>!</mo></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi></math> is equal to:
question: If the greatest value of the term independent of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mi>sin</mi><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>a</mi><mfrac><mrow><mi>cos</mi><mi>α</mi></mrow><mi>x</mi></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mn>10</mn></msup></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mn>10</mn><mo>!</mo></mrow><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>5</mn><mo>!</mo></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi></math> is equal to:
solution:
We have, rth term in the expansion of xsinα+acosαx10 is

Tr+1=Cr10xsinα10-racosαxr

r=0,1,2,…,10

Tr+1 will be independent of x, when 10-2r=0

⇒r=5

Now, T6=C510xsinα5×acosαx5

=C510×a5×125sin2α5

T6 will be greatest, when sin2α=1

Therefore, C510a525=C510

⇒a=2

q: The coefficient of <math><msup><mrow><mi>t</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msup></math> in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>t</mi><mn>6</mn></msup></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>t</mi></mrow></mfrac></mfenced><mn>3</mn></msup></math> is
question: The coefficient of <math><msup><mrow><mi>t</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msup></math> in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced separators="|"><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>t</mi><mn>6</mn></msup></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>t</mi></mrow></mfrac></mfenced><mn>3</mn></msup></math> is
solution:
1-t61-t3=1-t631-t-3

=1-t18-3t6+3t12 1-t-3

∴ Co-efficient of t4=1× 3+4-1C4= 6C4=15

q: Let the coefficients of the middle terms in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mfrac><mn>1</mn><msqrt><mn>6</mn></msqrt></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>4</mn></msup><mo>,</mo><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>β</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mi>β</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>6</mn></msup><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></math>, respectively form the first three terms of an A.P. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>d</mi></math> is the common difference of this A.P., then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>d</mi></mrow><msup><mi>β</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac></math> is equal to _____ .
question: Let the coefficients of the middle terms in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mfrac><mn>1</mn><msqrt><mn>6</mn></msqrt></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>4</mn></msup><mo>,</mo><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>β</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mi>β</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>6</mn></msup><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></math>, respectively form the first three terms of an A.P. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>d</mi></math> is the common difference of this A.P., then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>d</mi></mrow><msup><mi>β</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac></math> is equal to _____ .
solution:
Let the coefficients of the middle terms in the expansion of 16+βx4,1-3βx2 and 1-β2x6,β>0, respectively form the first three terms of an A.P. If d is the common difference of this A.P., then 50-2dβ2 is equal to _____ .

Given,

Coefficients of middle terms of given expansions 16+βx4, 1-3βx2 and1-β2x6 are C2416β2, C12-3β & C36-β23 respectively,

Now it is given that middle terms are forming an A.P

So, 2C12-3β = C36-β23+C2416β2

⇒2.2-3β=β2-5β32

⇒-24=2β-5β2

⇒5β2-2β-24=0

⇒5β2-12β+10β-24=0

⇒β5β-12+25β-12=0

⇒β+25β-12=0

So, β=125 and β=-2 {which is not possible as β>0}

Now d=C12-3β-C2416β2=-6β-β2

Now putting the value of β & d in 50-2dβ2 we get,

50-2dβ2=50-2-6β-β2β2=50+12β+2=57

subject: Mathematics
q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></math>, be the smallest number such that the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mfrac><mn>2</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></msup><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mn>30</mn></msup></math> has a term <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mi>α</mi></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>N</mi></math>. Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> is equal to _____ .
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn></math>, be the smallest number such that the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mfrac><mn>2</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></msup><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mn>30</mn></msup></math> has a term <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mi>α</mi></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>N</mi></math>. Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> is equal to _____ .
solution:
General term of the binomial expansion of x23+2x330 is

Tr+1=Cr30x2/330-r2x3r

⇒Tr+1=Cr30·2r·x60-11r3

So, for term of the form βx-α, we have

60-11r3<0

⇒11r>60

⇒r>6011⇒r=6

So,

T7=C630·26x-2

We have also observed β=C63026 is a natural number.

∴α=2

subject: Mathematics
q: Suppose <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>r</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow><mn>2023</mn></munderover><msup><mi>r</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>·</mo><mmultiscripts><mi>C</mi><mi>r</mi><none></none><mprescripts></mprescripts><none></none><mn>2023</mn></mmultiscripts><mo>=</mo><mn>2023</mn><mo>×</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>2</mn><mn>2022</mn></msup></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> is
question: Suppose <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>r</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow><mn>2023</mn></munderover><msup><mi>r</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>·</mo><mmultiscripts><mi>C</mi><mi>r</mi><none></none><mprescripts></mprescripts><none></none><mn>2023</mn></mmultiscripts><mo>=</mo><mn>2023</mn><mo>×</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>2</mn><mn>2022</mn></msup></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> is
solution:
Given,

∑r=02023r2·Cr2023=2023×α×22022

Now taking L.H.S we get,

∑r=02023r2·Cr2023=∑r=12023r2·2023r·Cr-12022

⇒∑r=02023r2·Cr2023=∑r=12023r·2023·Cr-12022

⇒∑r=02023r2·Cr2023=2023∑r=12023r-1+1·Cr-12022

⇒∑r=02023r2·Cr2023=2023∑r=12023r-1·Cr-12022+∑r=12023Cr-12022

⇒∑r=02023r2·Cr2023=20232022∑r=22023Cr-22021+∑r=12023Cr-12022

⇒∑r=02023r2·Cr2023=20232022×22021+22022

⇒∑r=02023r2·Cr2023=2023×220221011+1

⇒∑r=02023r2·Cr2023=2023×1012×22022

Now on comparing with ∑r=02023r2·Cr2023=2023×α×22022 we get,

α=1012

q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> denotes the sum of all the coefficients in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>10</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mi>n</mi></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> denotes the sum of all the coefficients in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mi>n</mi></msup></math>, then :
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> denotes the sum of all the coefficients in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>10</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mi>n</mi></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> denotes the sum of all the coefficients in the expansion of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mi>n</mi></msup></math>, then :
solution:
We have,

A=Sum of the coefficients in the expansion of

⇒1-3x+10x2n=(1-3+10)n=(8)n=(2)3n

(putting x=1)

Now, B=Sum of the coefficients in the expansion of

⇒1+x2n=(1+1)n=2n

Clearly, A=B3.

subject: Mathematics
q: The sum of all possible values of $\mathrm{n} \in \mathbf{N}$, so that the coefficients of $x, x^{2}$ and $x^{3}$ in the expansion of $\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{2}(1+x)^{\mathrm{n}}$, are in arithmetic progression is :
question: The sum of all possible values of $\mathrm{n} \in \mathbf{N}$, so that the coefficients of $x, x^{2}$ and $x^{3}$ in the expansion of $\left(1+x^{2}\right)^{2}(1+x)^{\mathrm{n}}$, are in arithmetic progression is :
solution:
$(1+x^2)^2 = 1 + 2x^2 + x^4$. Multiply with $(1+x)^n$:
Coefficient of $x$: $n$.
Coefficient of $x^2$: $\binom{n}{2} + 2 = \frac{n(n-1)}{2} + 2$.
Coefficient of $x^3$: $\binom{n}{3} + 2n = \frac{n(n-1)(n-2)}{6} + 2n$.
For AP: $2\left(\frac{n(n-1)}{2}+2\right) = n + \frac{n(n-1)(n-2)}{6} + 2n$.
Simplifying: $n^3 - 9n^2 + 26n - 24 = 0$.
$(n-2)(n-3)(n-4) = 0 \Rightarrow n = 2, 3, 4$.
Sum $= 2 + 3 + 4 = 9$.
